questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If ÷ means ×, × means +, + means – and – means ÷, find the value of 19 × 3 + 7 – 2 ÷ 6.
questions.solution:
19 + 3 - 7 ÷ 2 × 6

⇒  19 + 3 - 7 × 6
2

⇒ 22 - 21

⇒ 1


Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If ‘<’ means ‘minus’, ‘>’ means ‘plus’, ‘=’ means ‘multiplied by’ and ‘$’ means ‘divided by’ then what would be the value of 35 > 63 $ 7 < 4?
questions.solution:
35 + 63 ÷ 7 - 4

⇒ 35 + 9 - 4

⇒ 40.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If + means ÷, ÷ means –, – means × and × means +, then 16 + 8 ÷ 4 – 3 × 10 = ?
questions.solution:
16 ÷ 8 - 4 × 3 + 10

⇒ 2 - 12 + 10

⇒ 12 - 12 = 0.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If + means –, – means ×, ÷ means + and × means ÷, then 12 – 4 + 9 × 3 ÷ 3 = ?
questions.solution:
12 × 4 - 9 ÷ 3 + 3

⇒ 48 - 3 + 3
 
⇒ 48.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If × means ÷, – means ×, ÷ means + and + means –, then (5 – 17 ÷ 5) × 9 + 13 = ?
questions.solution:
(5 × 17 + 5) ÷ 9 - 13

⇒ 90 ÷ 9 - 13

⇒ 10 - 13

⇒ -3.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If × means –, + means ÷, – means × and ÷ means +, then 11 – 2 ÷ 700 + 70 × 50 = ?
questions.solution:
11 × 2 + 700 ÷ 70 - 50

⇒ 22 + 10 - 50

⇒ 32 - 50

⇒ - 18.

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If ‘+’ means ‘divided by’, ‘-‘ means ‘added to’, ‘×’ means ‘subtracted from’ and ‘÷’ means ‘multiplied by’, then what is the value of 17 ÷ 11 – 12 + 6?
questions.solution:
17 × 11 + 12 ÷ 6

⇒ 187 + 2

⇒ 189.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Which interchange of signs will make the following equation correct?
 
10 + 10 ÷ 10 – 10 × 10 = 10
questions.solution:
From the option 3rd, we get:

⇒  10 + 10 ÷ 10 – 10 × 10 = 10

⇒  10 × 10 ÷ 10 – 10 + 10 = 10

⇒  10 – 10 + 10 = 10.

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to replace * signs and to balance the following equation:

8 * 8 * 1 * 7 = 8
questions.solution:
From the option 3rd, we get:
 
8 * 8 * 1 * 7 = 8
 
⇒ 8 ÷ 8 × 1 + 7 = 8

⇒ 1 × 1 + 7 = 8

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If ‘R’ stands for ‘-‘, ‘A’ stands for ‘+’, ‘B’ stands for ‘÷’ and ‘C’ stands for ‘×’, then what is the value, of the given equation? (BODMAS rule will not be applicable)

25 A 37 C 2 B 4 R 1 = ?
questions.solution:
R = – , A = +, B = ÷, C = ×

25 A 37 C 2 B 4 R 1 = ?

⇒ ? = 25 + 37 × 2 ÷ 4 – 1

⇒ ? = 25 + 37/2 – 1

⇒ ? =  24 + 37/2

⇒ ? = 85/2 = 42.5

Hence, the option C is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
 If ‘–’ stands for ‘÷’; ‘+’ stands for ‘×’; ‘÷’ stands for ‘–’ and ‘×’ stands for ‘+’. Which one of the following is correct?
questions.solution:
– ⇒ ÷+ ⇒ ×
÷ ⇒ –× ⇒ +

From the option 1:

⇒ 10 + 5 – 5 ÷ 5 × 5 = 10
⇒ 10 × 5 ÷ 5 – 5 + 5 = 10
⇒ 10 × 1 – 5 + 5 = 10
⇒ 10 – 5 + 5 = 10

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If + means ÷, – means ×, × means +, ÷ means –, give the value for 45 + 9 – 3 × 15 ÷ 2 = ?
questions.solution:
+ ⇒ ÷– ⇒ ×
× ⇒ +÷ ⇒ –

45 + 9 – 3 × 15 ÷ 2 = ?

⇒ ? = 45 ÷ 9 × 3 + 15 – 2

⇒ ? = 5 × 3 + 15 – 2

⇒ ? = 15 + 15 – 2

⇒ ? = 30 – 2

⇒ ? = 28.

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Which interchange of signs will make the following equation correct?

(16 – 4) × 6 ÷ 2 + 8 = 30
questions.solution:
From the first option, we will get:

(16 – 4) × 6 ÷ 2 + 8 = 30
 
⇒ (16 ÷ 4) × 6 – 2 + 8 = 30
 
⇒ 4 × 6 – 2 + 8 = 30
 
⇒ 24 – 2 + 8 = 30
 
⇒ 32 – 2 = 30

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
25 * 2 * 6 = 4 * 11 * 0 

Which set of symbols can replace?
questions.solution:
From the first option, we get:
 
25 × 2 – 6 = 4 × 11 + 0
 
⇒ 50 – 6 = 44 + 0 
 
⇒ 44 = 44.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If × stands for –, + means ×, ÷ means + and – means ÷, then what is the value of the given expression ?

175 – 25 ÷ 5 + 20 × 3 + 10 = ?
questions.solution:
× ⇒ –+ ⇒ ×
÷ ⇒ +÷ ⇒ ÷

175 – 25 ÷ 5 + 20 × 3 + 10 = ?

⇒ ? = 175 ÷ 25 + 5 × 20 – 3 × 10

⇒ ? = 7 + 100 – 30

⇒ ? = 77

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If ‘×’ means ‘addition’, ‘–’ means ‘division’, ‘÷’ means subtraction and ‘+’ means ‘multiplication’, then which of the following equations is correct?
questions.solution:
From the first option, we get:

16 + 5 – 10 × 4 ÷ 3 = 9

⇒ 16 × 5 ÷ 10 + 4 – 3 = 9
 
⇒ 16 × 1+ 4 – 3 = 9
2

⇒ 8 + 4 – 3 = 9

⇒ 12 – 3 = 9.

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to replace * signs and to balance the given equation:

18 * 6 * 3 * 12 * 24
questions.solution:
From the second option, we get:

18 × 6 ÷ 3 – 12 = 24
 
⇒ 18 × 2 – 12 = 24
 
⇒ 36 – 12 = 24

Hence, the option B is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Which interchange of signs will make the following equation correct?

(8 – 8) + 8 × 32 = 64
questions.solution:
From the fourth option, we get:

(8 – 8) + 8 × 32 = 64

⇒  (8 + 8) ÷ 8 × 32 = 64

⇒ 16 ÷ 8 × 32 = 64

⇒ 2 × 32 = 64

Hence, the option D is correct.\
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
Which of the following interchange of sign would make the given equation correct?

64 – 8 × 9 ÷ 8 = 64
questions.solution:
From the option fourth, we get

64 – 8 × 9 ÷ 8 = 64

Putting (– & ÷) in the equation,

⇒ 64 ÷ 8 × 9 – 8 = 64

⇒ 8 × 9 – 8 = 64

⇒ 72 – 8 = 64

⇒ 64 = 64

Hence, the option D is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Directions: Kindly solve the following questions and choose an option as your answer for each.
If + stands for division; × stands for addition;  –  stands for multiplication; ÷ stands for subtraction, which of the following is correct?

1. 15 ÷ 5 × 2 – 6 + 3 = 28

2. 15 × 5 + 2 – 6 ÷ 3 = 56.5

3. 15 + 5 – 2 ÷ 6 × 3 = 3

4. 15 – 5 + 2 × 6 ÷ 3 = 41
questions.solution:
From the third equation, we get

15 + 5 – 2 ÷ 6 × 3 = 3

After interchanging the symbol

⇒ 15 ÷ 5 × 2 – 6 + 3 = 3

⇒ 3 × 2 – 6 + 3 = 3

⇒ 6 – 6 + 3 = 3

⇒ 3 = 3

Hence, the option A is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If \(9 \times 6=45,7 \times 4=33\) and \(6 \times 4=20\), then what is the value of \(5 \times 3\) ?
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If ' I ' stands for ' + ', 'J' stands for ' \(\times\) ', 'K' stands for ' \(\div\) ', and 'L' stands for ' - ', what will come in place of the question mark '?' in the following equation? \(38I(3 \mathrm{J}4) \mathrm{L}(16 \mathrm{~K} 2)I 11=\) ?
questions.solution:
Given Equation: \(38 \mathrm{I}(3 \mathrm{~J} 4) \mathrm{L}(16 \mathrm{~K} 2) I 11=\) ?

BODMAS Rule:

\(\begin{array}{|c|c|}

\hline \text { B } & \text { Brackets in order }(),\{ \},[] \\

\hline \text { O } & \text { of } \\

\hline \text { D } & \text { Division ( } \div \text { ) } \\

\hline \text { M } & \text { Multiplication (x) } \\

\hline \text { A } & \text { Addition (+) } \\

\hline \text { S } & \text { Subtraction (-) } \\

\hline

\end{array}\)

Decoding the given information:

\(\begin{array}{|l|c|c|c|c|}

\hline \text { Alphabet } & \text { I } & \text { J } & \text { K } & \text{L} \\

\hline \text { Meaning } & + & \times & \div & - \\

\hline

\end{array}\)

Now replacing the sign from left to right using BODMAS Rule, we get:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 38+(3 \times 4)-(16 \div 2)+11=? \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{38+12-8+11=?} \\

& \Rightarrow 61-8=53 .

\end{aligned}\)

Hence, the correct option is "Option 4".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If \(L>M, M=N\) and \(D < M\), then which of the following relations will be false?
questions.solution:
Given:
\(L>M, M=N\) and \(D < M\).
Option 1) \(\mathrm{L}>\mathrm{D} \rightarrow\) True. (As \(\mathrm{L}>\mathrm{M}\) and M D definitely holds.)
Option 2) \(M < L \rightarrow\) True. (\(L>M\) so the converse that is \(L>M\) definitely holds.)
Option 3) \(L>N \rightarrow\) True. \((L>M, M=N\), this implies that \(L>N\).)
Option 4) \(N < D \rightarrow\) False. (\(M=N\) and \(D < M\), this implies that \(N>D\).)
Therefore, only the fourth option is false.
Hence, the correct answer is "Option 4".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Select the correct combination of mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right in the blanks below to balance the given equation.

\(8 \_27 \_9 \_6 \_5=0\)
questions.solution:
Given equation: 8 _ 27 _ 9 _ \(6 \_5=0\)

Option 1) +, -, +, +

Mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 8+27-9+6+5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow 46-9=0 \\

& \Rightarrow 37=0

\end{aligned}\)

Option 2),\(+, \div,-,-\)

Mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 8+\underline{27 \div 9}-6-5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{8+3}-6-5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow 11 \underline{-6-5}=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{11-11}=0 \\

& \Rightarrow 0=0

\end{aligned}\)

Option 3),\(- ,\div,+,+\)

Mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 8-\underline{27 \div 9}+6+5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{8}-3+6+5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{19-3}=0 \\

& \Rightarrow 16=0

\end{aligned}\)

Option 4),\(-,+, \div,+\)

Mathematical signs to sequentially place from left to right:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 8-27+9 \div 6+5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{8}-27+1.5+5=0 \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{14.5-27}=0 \\

& \Rightarrow-12.5=0

\end{aligned}\)

Hence, the correct answer is ",\(+, \div,-,-\) ".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If + means \(\div, \times\) means,\(+, \div\) means \(-\&-\) means \(\times\), then, \(146+2-3 \div 123 \times 5-2=?\)
questions.solution:
The equation given is \(146+2-3 \div 123 \times 5-2=\) ?

\(\begin{array}{|c|c|}

\hline \text { Symbol } & \text { Means } \\

\hline + & \div \\

\hline \times & + \\

\hline \div & - \\

\hline- & \times \\

\hline

\end{array}\)

New equation after replacing symbols-

\(146 \div 2 \times 3-123+5 \times 2\)

According to the BODMAS rule-

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 146 \div 2 \times 3-123+5 \times 2 \\

& \Rightarrow 73 \times 3-123+10 \\

& \Rightarrow 219-123+10 \\

& \Rightarrow 229-123 \\

& =106

\end{aligned}\)

Hence, Option (2) is correct.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If A means, \(+ B\) means,\(- C\) means \(\times ~\&~ D\) means ÷, then what is the meaning of 100 D 20 C 3 A 10 B 5 ?
questions.solution: \(\begin{aligned} & 100 \text { D } 20 \text { C } 3 \text { A } 10 \text { B } 5 \\ & 100 \div 20 \times 3+10-5 \\ & =25-5=20 \text { (Changing the signs) }\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If '#' represents '\(-\)', ''\(@\)' represents '\(\times\)', '\(\$\)' represents '\(+\)' and '\(\%\)' represents '\(\div 1\)', then what is the value of \(20 ~\$~ 15\) \(\#\) 10 \(@\) \(45 ~\%~ 15\) ?
questions.solution:
Given Equation: 20 \(\$\) 15 \(\#\) 10 \(@\) \(45 ~\%~ 15=\) ?

Decoding the given information:

\(\begin{array}{|l|l|l|l|}

\hline \text { Symbol } & \# & @ & \$ \\

\hline \text { Meaning } & - & \times & + \\

\hline

\end{array}\)

Now replacing the sign from left to right using BODMAS Rule, we get:

\(\begin{aligned}

& \Rightarrow 20+15-10 \times \underline{45 \div 15}=? \\

& \Rightarrow 20+15-\underline{10 \times 3}=? \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{20+15}-30=? \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{\mathbf{3 5}-30}=\mathbf{5} .

\end{aligned}\)

Hence, the correct option is "Option 1".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
After interchanging the given two numbers and two signs what will be the values of equation (I) and (II) respectively?

\(\times\) and +, + 3 and 9

I. \(7 \times 9-8 \div 2+3\)

II. \(4 \times 9-3+8 \div 2\)
questions.solution:
According to the question, after interchanging the given two signs and two numbers i.e :

- \(\times\) and +

- Two numbers 3 and 9

So,

\(\begin{aligned}

& \text {I. } 7+3-\underline{8 \div 2} \times 9 \\

& \Rightarrow 7+3-\underline{4 \times 9} \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{7+3-36} \\

& \Rightarrow \underline{10-36} \\

& \Rightarrow-26

\end{aligned}\)

II. \(4+3-9 \times \underline{8 \div 2}\)

\(\Rightarrow 4+3-\underline{9 \times 4}\)

\(\Rightarrow \underline{4+3}-36\)

\(\Rightarrow \underline{7-36}\)

\(\Rightarrow-29\)

Here, values of equation (I) and (II) are (-26) and (-29) respectively.

Hence, "Option - (2)" is the correct answer.
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q:
Which two numbers should be interchanged to make the given equation correct?

\(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

(Note: Interchange should be done of entire number and not individual digits of a given number.)
questions.solution:
Given equation: \(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

Let's check each option one by one.

Option 1) 4 and 2

Given: \(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

After interchanging the numbers we get;

\(\begin{aligned}

& \rightarrow 21 \times 2+(84 \div 7) \times 4-15 \times 5+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow 42+12 \times 4-15 \times 5+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow 42+48-75+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow 132-75=105 \\

& \rightarrow 57 \neq 105

\end{aligned}\)

Option 2) 84 and 42

Given: \(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

After interchanging the numbers we get;

\(\begin{aligned}

& \rightarrow 21 \times 4+(42 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+84=105 \\

& \rightarrow 21 \times 4+6 \times 2-15 \times 5+84=105 \\

& \rightarrow 84+12-75+84=105 \\

& \rightarrow 180-75=105 \\

& \rightarrow \mathbf{1 0 5}=\mathbf{1 0 5}

\end{aligned}\)

Option 3) 4 and 5

Given: \(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

After interchanging the numbers we get;

\(\begin{aligned}

& \rightarrow 21 \times 5+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 4+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow \mathbf{2 1} \times 5+\mathbf{1 2} \times \mathbf{2}-\mathbf{1 5} \times \mathbf{4}+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow \mathbf{1 0 5}+\mathbf{2 4}-60+\mathbf{4 2}=105 \\

& \rightarrow 171-60=105 \\

& \rightarrow 111 \neq 105

\end{aligned}\)

Option 4) 7 and 21

Given: \(21 \times 4+(84 \div 7) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105\)

After interchanging the numbers we get;

\(\begin{aligned}

& \rightarrow 7 \times 4+(84 \div 21) \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow 7 \times 4+4 \times 2-15 \times 5+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow \mathbf{2 8 + 8}-75+42=105 \\

& \rightarrow 78-75=105 \\

& \rightarrow 3 \neq 105

\end{aligned}\)

Hence, the correct answer is "Option 2".
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide

questions.subject: Logical Reasoning
questions.examName: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
questions.q: If \(20-10\) means \(200,8 \div 4\) means \(12,6 \times 2\) means 4 and \(12+3\) means 4 then \(100-10 \times 1000 \div 1000+100 \times 10-\) ?
questions.solution:
As per the question '-' means 'x'; ' \(\div\) ' means '+' ; 'x' mean '-'; '+' means ' \(\div\) ' put these in the question

\(\begin{aligned}

& =100 \times 10-1000+1000 \div 100-10 \\

& =1000-1000+10-10=0

\end{aligned}\)
questions.source: English and Logical Reasoning Prep Guide
